title: Wales: Upper Cantref Farm, Brecon (May half-term)

unresolved.excerpt: Thomas, William + Peter set off on a day trip. It didn't stop raining. At 2.40. they came back without having stopped for a picnic lunch.

unresolved.issue: Joan Clarke's location not stated. The diary mentions cancelling a barbeque and inviting themselves to tea on Tuesday; they drove there after packing up camp and before reaching Malvern. Could be anywhere between Brecon and Malvern.

summary: May half-term 1995 camping trip for all seven family members to Upper Cantref Farm near Brecon in the Brecon Beacons, Wales. The four-night stay (Saturday to Tuesday) featured horse riding for the whole family, day trips into Brecon, and a supper at the White Swan in Llanfrynach. The return journey stopped for fish and chips in Malvern, where Thomas was dropped off.
extraction_notes: Pages have mixed_start and mixed_end: A_0150_R begins mid-page continuing from a previous trip (Grasmere/Lake District content on the same page before Saturday May 27th heading), and A_0152_L ends mid-page. No route sketch. The date 1995-05-27 for Saturday May 27th is confirmed by calendar (May 27, 1995 was indeed a Saturday); 1995-05-30 for Tuesday 30th May is given explicitly in the transcription. The village '[?Llangynach]' is uncertain — Llangynidr is near Upper Cantref Farm but the transcription spelling differs. Joan Clarke's address is unknown. The boys' Sunday day trip destination is never named. The Roman Bathhouse near Llanfrynach is likely the remains of the Roman fort/baths at Brecon Gaer or the bathhouse at Y Gaer — recorded as approximate.
